# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:latest

# Install required packages
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y \
    nginx \
    curl \
    python3-pip \
    redis-server \
    postgresql-server postgresql-contrib && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set up PostgreSQL database
RUN service postgresql start && \
    psql -U postgres -c "CREATE DATABASE mydatabase" && \
    psql -U postgres -d mydatabase -f /path/to/your/schema.sql && \
    service postgresql stop

# Configure Nginx for web server
COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
RUN mkdir -p /var/www/html && \
    echo "Welcome to the Web Server" > /var/www/html/index.html

# Expose necessary ports
EXPOSE 80 8080 5432 6379

# Set environment variables
ENV POSTGRES_DB=mydatabase \
    POSTGRES_USER=postgres \
    POSTGRES_PASSWORD=secret \
    REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379 \
    API_SERVER_URL=http://api-server:8080

# Start services
CMD ["sh", "-c", "service postgresql start && redis-server & nginx -g 'daemon off;'"]
